# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: Rapid Build & Deploy (Containerized App)

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch: # Allows manual trigger

jobs:
  build-and-push-image:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write # Required for pushing to GitHub Packages, optional for other registries
      id-token: write # Required for OIDC-based authentication (e.g., AWS ECR, GCP GCR)

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

    - name: Log in to Container Registry # Example: Docker Hub. Adjust for your registry (e.g., ECR, GCR, GHCR)
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_USERNAME }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }}
        # If using GitHub Container Registry (GHCR):
        # registry: ghcr.io
        # username: ${{ github.actor }}
        # password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Build and push Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        push: true
        tags: |
          your-org/your-app:latest
          your-org/your-app:${{ github.sha }} # Tag with commit SHA for immutability
        cache-from: type=gha # Use GitHub Actions cache for faster subsequent builds
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push-image # This job depends on a successful image build and push
    environment: production # Optional: GitHub Environments for deployment tracking
    permissions:
      id-token: write # Required for OIDC-based authentication to cloud providers

    steps:
    - name: Configure AWS credentials (Example: EKS/ECS deployment)
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
      with:
        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}:role/github-actions-deploy-role
        aws-region: us-east-1

    - name: Update Kubernetes Deployment (Example: EKS)
      # This is a simplified example. In a real-world scenario, you might use Helm, Kustomize, ArgoCD, etc.
      run: |
        # Install kubectl if not present on the runner (it usually is on ubuntu-latest)
        # curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
        # chmod +x kubectl && sudo mv kubectl /usr/local/bin/

        # Update Kubeconfig if necessary (e.g., for EKS clusters)
        # aws eks update-kubeconfig --name your-eks-cluster-name --region us-east-1

        # Rollout a new image to your deployment
        kubectl set image deployment/your-app your-container=your-org/your-app:${{ github.sha }} -n your-app-namespace
        kubectl rollout status deployment/your-app -n your-app-namespace

    - name: Verify Deployment (Optional but Recommended)
      run: |
        echo "Deployment to production environment completed successfully with image: your-org/your-app:${{ github.sha }}"
        # Add basic health checks or smoke tests here if desired
        # curl -sS https://your-app.your-domain.com/health || exit 1
